Advanced Capabilities
#  Control, Optimise, and Explore with Triple Alpha
Beyond higher-level programming tools, Triple Alpha gives developers access to advanced capabilities supported by Horizon Quantum’s underlying execution infrastructure. 
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/ablwYbbci2UF6Iqv_hero-advanced-capabilities.jpg?auto=format&q=100)
###  Powerful capabilities in a single development environment
Advanced capabilities expand Triple Alpha’s reach. They enable developers to experiment with low-level hardware control, resource optimization, and alternative computational models that extend the limits of today’s quantum devices—all within a single development environment.
Program optimization
Measurement-based quantum computation
Dynamic memory management
Pulse control
[![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYJptN0YXLCxVVNa_card-illustration-optimisation.png?auto=format,compress) Identify improvements across circuit fragments Triple Alpha’s optimization engine improves the efficiency of code according to user-specified metrics. It automatically identifies simplifications within circuit fragments constructed from any instruction set, including group-theoretic reductions like Euler decompositions and composition of non-unitary effects such as dephasing and decoherence.  Learn more  ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/product/advanced-capabilities/program-optimization)
Program optimization
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYJptN0YXLCxVVNa_card-illustration-optimisation.png?auto=format,compress)
####  Identify improvements across circuit fragments
Triple Alpha’s optimization engine improves the efficiency of code according to user-specified metrics. It automatically identifies simplifications within circuit fragments constructed from any instruction set, including group-theoretic reductions like Euler decompositions and composition of non-unitary effects such as dephasing and decoherence. 
[Learn more ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/product/advanced-capabilities/program-optimization)
Measurement-based quantum computation
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYJps90YXLCxVVNZ_card-illustration-mbqc.png?auto=format,compress)
####  Transform gate-based programs to measurement-based quantum computation
Unlike the circuit model, where quantum gates are applied sequentially, MBQC performs computation through measurements on entangled quantum states—an approach well-suited to photonic hardware and privacy-preserving protocols such as blind quantum computing. Triple Alpha’s MBQC compilation capability converts gate-based quantum programs into this measurement-driven form.
[Learn more ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/product/advanced-capabilities/measurement-based-quantum-computation)
Dynamic memory management
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYJpsd0YXLCxVVNY_card-illustration-dmm.png?auto=format,compress)
####  Maximize quantum resource efficiency through reusability
The equivalent of classical malloc() and free() functions, this capability allows programmers to dynamically allocate and release qubits within their quantum programs. Qubits that are no longer in use can be repurposed, dramatically reducing the total quantum memory required for complex computations and unlocking greater resource efficiency.
[Learn more ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/product/advanced-capabilities/dynamic-memory-management)
Pulse control
![""](https://images.prismic.io/horizon-v2/aYJptd0YXLCxVVNb_card-illustration-pulse-control.png?auto=format,compress)
####  Manipulate quantum processors at the physical level
Triple Alpha gives developers direct, low-level control over the pulses sent to a quantum processor, enabling custom operations that bypass native gate sets. By shaping pulses to match device-specific behavior, users can reduce errors, improve fidelity, and finely tune hardware performance across different quantum systems. 
[Learn more ](https://www.horizonquantum.com/product/advanced-capabilities/pulse-control)
###  Learn more

Register for early access
###  Interested in exploring our technology? Register for early access to Triple Alpha
We’re gradually expanding availability and inviting selected users to try our tools. As we continue to release new capabilities, we’re onboarding participants in stages and giving priority to those with compelling use cases. Register for early access to Triple Alpha using the link below.
